title: Ariadne Operation Registry v2
description: Schema for config/operations.v2.json: the additive, superset operation registry that unifies the two existing v1 surfaces (the 29 wired ops in schemas/cad_job.schema.json + config/cad_job_operation_aliases.json, and the 480 catalog ops in config/autocad_native_arx_operation_catalog.json) into one record-per-canonical-op_id table. v2 is EXTEND-ONLY: it never removes/renames a v1 enum op (those must appear here with status implemented|wired), and it references — does not regenerate — the frozen v1 alias map (derived_from). The registry is the runtime source of truth for which operations exist and whether they are runnable; the no-fake-success invariant is encoded as data via status (blocked|not_implemented|deprecated) and the explicit host_eligibility/engine_tier crosswalk. additionalProperties true on operation records for forward extension.

generated_at.description: ISO date the registry was generated.

derived_from.description: Source artifacts this registry joins/derives from (referenced, not regenerated). Expected to include config/cad_job_operation_aliases.json, config/autocad_native_arx_operation_catalog.json, schemas/cad_job.schema.json.

host_class_crosswalk.description: The main new intelligence v2 adds: an explicit crosswalk between the two distinct 4-value vocabularies that already coexist — execution_host_class (WHERE an op runs: dbx|coreconsole|arx_adapter|full_autocad, from the alias map) and engine_tier (WHAT API surface an op needs: native_arx_only|objectdbx_capable|managed_also|accoreconsole_lisp_also, from the catalog). They are NOT the same axis. This object documents, per engine_tier, the ordered (cheapest-host-first) host_eligibility list and whether a managed handler exists, so the router can pick the lowest-capability host and fall back.

by_engine_tier.description: Map engine_tier -> { host_eligibility[], managed_handler_available }. Recommended defaults: objectdbx_capable -> [dbx, coreconsole, arx_adapter, full_autocad]; accoreconsole_lisp_also -> [coreconsole, dbx, arx_adapter, full_autocad]; native_arx_only -> [arx_adapter, full_autocad]; managed_also -> managed_handler_available true.

totals.description: Optional roll-up counts (by status, by family, by engine_tier) for quick auditing.

operations.description: One record per canonical operation id (the union of the 29 wired ids and the 480 catalog op_ids; the 6 exact-mapped ids are counted once). Wired ops MUST carry status implemented|wired; catalog-only ops default to catalogued.

engine_tier.description: catalog engine_tier vocabulary (WHAT API surface an op needs).

id.description: Canonical operation id. For wired ops this is the v1 enum operation (e.g. write.entity.line); for catalog-only ops this is the catalog op_id (e.g. command.invoke.coroutine).

family.description: Operation family. For catalog ops, the catalog family (one of the 16, e.g. entities, custom_objects_protocols, reactors_events). For router-synthetic ops, a router family label.

status.description: Reality of this op. implemented = has a live, test-asserted handler. wired = route exists but coverage is incomplete. stub = intentionally returns NOT_IMPLEMENTED. catalogued = known from the catalog but no router path yet. deprecated = explicitly not used. blocked = cannot run because a required host/tier/license/environment is unavailable. Per no-fake-success, anything not actually runnable MUST be catalogued|stub|blocked|deprecated, never implemented/wired.

blocked_reason.description: Required when status == blocked (e.g. 'requires full_autocad host not currently available').

engine_tier.description: The catalog engine_tier for this op (WHAT API surface). Drives host_eligibility via host_class_crosswalk.

host_eligibility.description: Ordered (cheapest-host-first) list of execution_host_class values this op can run on. Derived from engine_tier + execution_context. The router picks the first available host and falls back down the list.

execution_context.description: Free-text catalog execution_context (e.g. hostless_dbx_in_accoreconsole, in_process_host) carried through for reference.

write_level.description: Write semantics mapped to the v1 write_mode vocabulary. default_write_mode is what the op uses unless overridden; allowed_write_modes bounds what a job may request. Derived from verb prefix + catalog dwg_persisted.

dwg_persisted.description: Catalog dwg_persisted flag: whether the op's effect is persisted to the DWG.

handler.description: How the op is dispatched. router_lane names the router entrypoint; dispatcher_symbol is the C++/C# handler name where one exists (populated for the 29 wired ops from the names existing native_arx_dbx tests already assert, so a future test can cross-check registry<->source; null for catalogued ops). native_api carries the catalog's native API signature. composed_of lists the catalog op_ids a synthetic/alias router op is built from (mirrors the v1 alias map catalog_reference_ids).

router_lane.description: Router entrypoint/lane that handles this op. null for catalogued ops with no lane yet.

dispatcher_symbol.description: C++/C# handler symbol (e.g. appendLine, createLayout, runLineJigProbe). Non-null only for ops with a real handler; null for catalogued ops.

native_api.description: Native API signature from the catalog (e.g. acedCommandC(...)).

execution_host_class.description: Preferred/primary host class for this op (the alias map's execution_host_class for wired ops). host_eligibility carries the full fallback list.

composed_of.description: For alias/synthetic router ops: the catalog op_ids this aggregate is composed of. Each MUST resolve in config/autocad_native_arx_operation_catalog.json. Mirrors the v1 alias map's catalog_reference_ids.

mapping_type.description: Relationship to the catalog (carried from the v1 alias map). exact = id is itself a catalog op_id (the 6 exact ops). alias = router id aliases one catalog op. synthetic = router aggregate over multiple catalog ops.

catalog_op_id.description: The catalog op_id when mapping_type == exact (equals id); null for alias/synthetic (their constituents are in handler.composed_of).

input_schema.description: Reference to the request-args validator for this op (e.g. schemas/cad_job.v2.schema.json#/allOf for wired ops, or an op-specific args schema).

output_schema.description: Reference to the result validator for this op (e.g. schemas/cad_result.v2.schema.json, or schemas/dwg_geometry_extract.schema.json for the extract lane).

summary.description: Human description (from the catalog summary where applicable).

evidence_refs.description: Paths/ids of evidence that this op behaves as declared (run artifacts, test names, extract JSONs). Required to justify an implemented/wired status beyond the catalog.
